message.content:
Test file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_10-10-59_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: "should apply 20% loyalty discount for long-standing customer (>= 2 years)"
Expected: { premium: 95 } for 2-year customer with single plain sword
Implementation file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_10-10-59_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/claim-office.ts

Two changes needed:
1. Rename "newcomer surcharge" → "first-insurance surcharge", apply UNCONDITIONALLY (always +10% per item base premium), since the spec says "each item in a quote is treated as a first insurance, regardless of customer history".
2. Add loyalty discount: -20% × item base premium for each item when customer.yearsWithMHPCO >= 2.

Mathematically identical: -20% × policy_base = sum over items of (-20% × item_base). So apply per-item to keep implementation simple.

Both apply to ITEM BASE PREMIUM (not the post-surcharge value). Use the existing surcharge(base, applies, rate) helper. Loyalty rate = -0.2 (a negative "surcharge" / discount).

For component groups (rune blocks), the "base" used for the per-item surcharge in the existing code is the GROUP base, not individual item bases. So loyalty -20% × group_base. That's still policy-base-wise correct (since policy_base = sum of group bases + sum of non-component item bases).

Verify all 19 tests still pass after this change:
- For loyalty test (2-year cust, plain sword): 100 + 10 (first-ins always) - 20 (loyalty) + 5 fee = 95 ✓
- All earlier tests have yearsWithMHPCO=0 (newcomers, no loyalty). The renamed "first-ins" with same +10% rate applied unconditionally yields the same result (since newcomers also got +10%).

Add constants: FIRST_INSURANCE_SURCHARGE_RATE = 0.1; LOYALTY_DISCOUNT_RATE = -0.2; LOYALTY_THRESHOLD_YEARS = 2.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: I'll implement the minimal changes to make the loyalty discount test pass.
Now I'll implement the minimal changes:

content.content: The file /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_10-10-59_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/claim-office.ts has been updated successfully.

input.new_string:
const isLongStandingCustomer = (customer: Customer): boolean =>
  customer.yearsWithMHPCO >= LOYALTY_THRESHOLD_YEARS;

content.text:
All 19 tests pass.

🟢 Green Phase Complete:
**Implementation**: 
1. Renamed `NEWCOMER_PER_ITEM_SURCHARGE_RATE` to `FIRST_INSURANCE_SURCHARGE_RATE` (same +0.1 rate) and applied it unconditionally (passing `true` as the `applies` flag) in both `premiumForItem` and `componentGroupTotal`.
2. Replaced `isNewcomer` helper with `isLongStandingCustomer` (yearsWithMHPCO >= 2).
3. Added loyalty discount as a negative "surcharge" (`LOYALTY_DISCOUNT_RATE = -0.2`) applied per-item / per-component-group when customer is long-standing.
4. Added constants `LOYALTY_DISCOUNT_RATE` and `LOYALTY_THRESHOLD_YEARS`.

**Result**: All 19 tests now pass (30 todo).

**Approach**: Reused the existing `surcharge(base, applies, rate)` helper for both the unconditional first-insurance surcharge and the loyalty discount (as a negative rate). For the loyalty test: 100 base + 10 (first-ins) - 20 (loyalty) + 5 fee = 95 ✓. All earlier newcomer tests are mathematically unchanged: previously the +10% applied only when `isNewcomer` (yearsWithMHPCO === 0); now it always applies, but those tests all had yearsWithMHPCO=0 with no loyalty discount, yielding identical results.

Files modified:
- `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_10-10-59_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/claim-office.ts`

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
